description.value:
You deconstruct one magic item you touch with a maximum rarity of Common which is not cursed and does not have a specific condition for its destruction. If the item is being worn or carried by another creature, they can make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. On a success, the item is not destroyed and the spell is wasted.

When you destroy the item, you can choose to make an Intelligence (Artificer's Tools) check as a reaction, with a DC determined by consulting the Magic Item Destruction table. On a success, you gain enough information during the process to create a formula for the item, allowing you to craft additional copies in the future.

When the item is destroyed, it is transmuted into Residuum dust with a value equal to half the destroyed item's value. You can collect this dust if the item is destroyed under careful laboratory conditions.

Magic Item Destruction
RarityDCSpell Level
Common111st level
Uncommon152nd level
Rare193rd level
Very Rare234th level
Legendary275th level

At Higher Levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can deconstruct items of higher rarity, as shown in the Magic Item Destruction table above.
